tool_args.label: MTA Real Estate Finance Committee Agenda Items - December 2023

payload.title: DECEMBER 2023 MTA REAL ESTATE FINANCE COMMITTEE AGENDA ITEMS
payload.content_type: meeting agenda and staff summary packet

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: GCT Short-Term Retail Licensing Program Modification

payload.project_name: MODIFICATION TO THE GRAND CENTRAL TERMINAL SHORT-TERM RETAIL LICENSING PROGRAM
payload.description: Expansion of the GCT Short-Term Retail Licensing Program to include Retail Kiosks throughout GCT, sunsets Graybar Retail Kiosk Policy, removes rental range, allows flexible RMU term durations of no more than 36 months
payload.location: Grand Central Terminal, New York, NY

location_normalized.raw_text: Grand Central Terminal, New York, NY

tool_args.label: Rail Control Center Annex at 806 9th Avenue

payload.project_name: Construction of the Rail Control Center Annex at 806 9th Avenue, Manhattan
payload.description: Construction and delivery of 30,000 SF of core-and-shell office space and 15 below-grade facility parking spaces within an affordable housing development on the site of the RCC parking lot

Mixed-use development of the Eastern Rail Yard and Western Rail Yard of the LIRR's John D. Caemmerer West Side Yard, including 99-year ground leases, affordable housing, PS/IS school, cultural facility
payload.location: John D. Caemmerer West Side Yard, Manhattan (10th to 12th Aves, 30th to 33rd Streets)

location_normalized.raw_text: John D. Caemmerer West Side Yard, Manhattan (10th to 12th Aves, 30th to 33rd Streets)

payload.description: Alternative location for Jamaica Bus Terminal; swing space at 168-25 Jamaica Avenue for bus operations support during relocation
Acquisition of permanent easements from NYC Parks at Hutchinson River Parkway Extension in support of the Penn Station Access Project, including Co-op City Station western egress
payload.location: Bronx, NY (Hutchinson River Parkway Extension and Co-op City Station)

location_normalized.raw_text: Bronx, NY (Hutchinson River Parkway Extension and Co-op City Station)

payload.project_name: Accessibility Improvements at NYCT's Broadway Junction Station
payload.description: Capital project to install accessibility improvements at the Broadway Junction subway station complex serving A, C, J, Z, and L lines, including station headhouse expansion, elevators, ramps, and new aerial bridges. Requires easements from Parks' Callahan-Kelly Playground.

tool_args.label: Original GCT Short-Term Licensing Policy adopted

payload.description: MTA Board approved the policy authorizing MTA Real Estate to enter into short-term license agreements in Grand Central Terminal for up to two years
GCT Development launched the Graybar Retail Kiosk Program in Grand Central Terminal's Graybar Passage
Estimated completion and delivery of the RCC Annex core-and-shell unit at 806 9th Avenue
MTA Board authorized conditional designation of Related/Goldman as developer for Western and Eastern Rail Yards
2020-2024 MTA Capital Program contains $20 million for the construction of the RCC Annex
Current estimate for the RCC Annex (Developer scope) reflecting escalation of construction costs and rise of interest rates
Net Present Value of Related/Goldman combined yard proposal (incorporating residual values) at 6% discount rate
WRY Initial Land Value
ERY Initial Land Value
Fair market value of the Surrendered Area at Gowanus Property
Total compensation for surrender of Gowanus Property area

tool_args.label: GCT Short Term Licensing Policy successful

payload.claim_text: The GCT Short Term Licensing Policy has been successfully used in GCT as a post-Covid approach to quickly activate vacant retail spaces while providing time to develop long-term plans for an evolving retail market
payload.subject: GCT Short Term Licensing Policy

tool_args.label: 15 new or renewed license agreements in GCT

payload.claim_text: The information submitted to the Finance Committee in April, 2022 reported 15 new or renewed license agreements completed in Grand Central Terminal which contributed to re-populating the retail environment in a post-Covid world
payload.subject: GCT Short Term Licensing Policy

payload.description: Compensation for temporary and permanent easements from Parks for Broadway Junction accessibility improvements
Shortfall in parkland replacement value after valuing easement areas and Sackman Street transfer

payload.raw_value_text: Year 1 $15,000 plus 10% sales above $500K; Year 5 $16,900 plus 10% sales above $500K
payload.description: Annual compensation for Rosen's Café LLC license at 2 Broadway

issues: metric_claim payload with raw_value_text should include normalized numeric value or value_min/value_max

tool_args.label: History of GCT Retail Licensing amendments

payload.claim_text: The GCT Short-Term Retail Licensing Policy was adopted April 27, 2009 and amended July 22, 2020, September 23, 2020, June 29, 2022, and September 21, 2022
payload.subject: GCT Short-Term Retail Licensing Policy

payload.description: The December 2023 modification is an expansion of the original GCT Short-Term Retail Licensing Program first adopted in 2009

tool_args.label: GCT modification sunsets Graybar Kiosk Policy

payload.description: The December 2023 modification sunsets the Graybar Retail Kiosk Policy and incorporates RMUs into the GCT Short Term Licensing Policy
